fields.description:
## Context

The Jira<->local reconciler (`src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/`) has accumulated 23 hard-won parity lessons (L1-L23), each a reactive fix to an observed drift. Parity was claimed ENUMERATIVELY (fix the field, claim parity for it), so drift kept appearing in the un-enumerated states BETWEEN the point-fixes. A 2026-07 audit comparing Jira REB (606 issues) vs the local store (600 tickets, 606 bindings) found four un-healed drift classes that `bridge-fsck` reports as clean:

- **A (444d)** local archive/delete never propagated to the bound Jira issue.
- **B (5854)** Jira-native issues never adopted at steady state.
- **C (13eb)** confirmed bindings to deleted Jira issues never GC'd when the local ticket is archived.
- **D (8de5)** `bridge-fsck` blind to binding-level drift.

Unified root cause: the reconciler is EDGE-triggered and driven from the wrong index (the active local snapshot + `prev_snapshot`) instead of LEVEL-triggered from the binding store. The invariants this work must preserve are documented in ADRs 0026-0029.

## Scope (RE-SCOPED 2026-07-03)

This epic's acceptance is the **landed in-repo code deliverable**: the level-triggered classifier + per-binding baseline + convergence property tests + the four drift-class heals (A/B/C/D) + safety rails + the Phase-1 rollout derisk (dual-write shadow, breaker enforcement, rollback docs) — all merged to main through Gerrit and unit-tested. The **operational rollout tail** (every-mode decision census breadth, the high-risk Phase-3 prev_snapshot->baseline consumer-flag flip, and its gate of >= N clean live shadow passes + the capped live heal of the 7 known instances) requires live production Jira and the deployed 20-min workflow, so it is carved to follow-up **lofty-beech-twang (a118)**, owned by the deployed-workflow operator. The full rollout design (Phases 1-4, ADRs 0026-0029) is preserved there.

## Success Criteria

- Reconciliation is expressed as a LEVEL-TRIGGERED controller with a per-binding baseline; CONVERGENCE to a fixed point is a TESTED PROPERTY, not a claim.
- All four drift classes (A/B/C/D) are healed and cannot recur (regression cells).
- Every existing lesson L1-L23 is preserved (no regression); the corrected P4 KEEPS the three-way-merge baseline (ADR 0026).
- Prior-art safety rails (confirm-before-destroy, circuit breaker, quarantine, metrics) are in place.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] A pure classifier `classify(local, jira, binding, baseline) -> Decision` encodes the full state matrix (local x jira x binding); every cell defined and table-tested. — landed on main.
- [ ] A per-binding last-synced BASELINE replaces `prev_snapshot`'s merge role WITHOUT removing direction arbitration (preserves L1 / commit `16870dbec`; the corrected P4). The corrupt-baseline fail-closed abort is preserved. — baseline machinery landed; the live consumer-flag flip is carved to a118.
- [ ] Convergence property test: apply the classifier's Decisions, then re-reconcile -> ZERO actions, from randomized states (catches A/B/C). Plus no-orphan, conservation, and direction-preservation (a teammate's Jira-side edit is never clobbered) properties. — landed on main.
- [ ] Multi-pass stateful + fault-injection tests that faithfully persist the baseline between passes (the root of B) and assert self-heal on partial failure. — landed on main.
- [ ] One classifier, TWO consumers: the live pass acts; `bridge-fsck` (offline, reads `bindings.json`) + `reconcile_check` (online) report (heals D). — landed on main.
- [ ] Safety rails: GC/terminal only on confirmed-404 grace (never snapshot-absence, L13/L16/L17); circuit breaker refuses a pass mutating/retiring > N% of bindings; per-binding quarantine; per-pass metrics. — landed on main.
- [ ] All six children closed with their guardrails met; ADRs 0026-0029 landed. Execution order: FOUNDATION amiss-bun-wince (baf7) FIRST -> the four drift-class heals (A=port-jar-withe/444d, B=undo-grape-frost/5854, C=false-topaz-prong/13eb, D=hale-cameo-basin/8de5) -> ROLLOUT soar-quill-flare (7d23) LAST. — done: all six children closed (7d23 closed on its re-scoped code deliverable).
- [ ] Rollout behind the mode ladder (reconcile-check report -> dry-run -> bootstrap -> live) with a shadow phase; the workflow_dispatch mode input on .github/workflows/reconcile-bridge.yml is the rollback brake. — mode ladder + shadow + rollback brake landed; live promotion is an operator action tracked on a118.
- [ ] FROZEN-ARTIFACT CENSUS REPLAY TEST (derisk 2026-07-03): a regression test replays the classifier over sanitized frozen production artifacts (614 bindings / 614 issues) and asserts EXACTLY the 7 known acting decisions (5x TERMINAL_TRANSITION REB-464/456/465/466/457, 1x PROBE/GC REB-530, 1x ADOPT REB-532) and nothing else, then asserts fixed-point on re-classify after simulated heal. — landed on main.
- [ ] OPERATIONAL TAIL carved to follow-up lofty-beech-twang (a118), owned by the deployed-workflow operator: every-mode decision-census breadth; the Phase-3 consumer-flag flip gated on >= N clean live shadow passes; the capped live heal of the 7 known drift instances with next-pass census = 0. Not in this epic's in-session scope. — carved out; provenance preserved via discovered_from 7d23.

## Notes

Prior art surveyed: Kubernetes controllers / Flux / Argo CD / Terraform / Crossplane (level-triggered reconcile, prune-off-by-default, `allowEmpty` mass-delete guard) and Unito / Exalate (adopt-by-match, provenance echo suppression, per-field 3-way merge, quarantine). This epic converts enumerative parity CLAIMS into a tested convergence PROPERTY.

## Derisk evidence (2026-07-03, pre-implementation)

Offline experiments on live production artifacts PASSED the E1 go/no-go: drift census = exactly the 7 known instances (zero unknown), acting rate 1.14% (8.8x headroom under a 10% breaker), lifecycle-matrix converges to fixed point in one simulated pass, and per-binding baselines are 100% seedable from prev_snapshot (all 5 mirrored fields present for all 613 bound+present keys). bridge-fsck confirmed blind (exit 0, "No issues found") over the same store. Full results + prior-art validation (k8s/Terraform/Entra/GCDS/Exalate) in this epic's comments.
